autoscaling_config.description: Configuration for instance autoscaling, including settings for scaling based on load. Constraints may depend on instance type and region.
autoscaling_config.type: list(object({asymmetric_autoscaling_options: list(object({overrides: list(object({autoscaling_limits: list(object({max_nodes: number,min_nodes: number}))})),replica_selection: list(object({location: string}))})),autoscaling_limits: list(object({max_nodes: optional(number),max_processing_units: optional(number),min_nodes: optional(number),min_processing_units: optional(number)})),autoscaling_targets: list(object({high_priority_cpu_utilization_percent: optional(number),storage_utilization_percent: optional(number)}))}))

config.description: Defines the configuration for the Spanner instance, including the regional or multi-regional instance placement and replication settings.

default_backup_schedule_type.description: Defines the default backup schedule type for the Spanner instance, specifying how backups are automatically managed.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the Spanner instance, limited to 30 characters.

edition.description: Specifies the edition of the Spanner instance, such as 'STANDARD' or 'ENTERPRISE'; must match the available editions for your project and region.

force_destroy.description: Indicates whether the Spanner instance should be forcefully destroyed, removing all data and resources without confirmation.

labels.description: Key-value pairs used to organize and categorize the Spanner instance; must comply with label naming conventions.

name.description: Unique name for the Spanner instance, must be between 6 and 30 characters and match the pattern [a-z]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?.

num_nodes.description: Specifies the number of nodes allocated to the Spanner instance, which affects performance and cost. Must be a positive integer.

processing_units.description: The number of processing units to allocate for the Spanner instance; must be a positive integer and adhere to platform-specific minimum and maximum limits.

timeouts.description: Specifies the duration to wait for each operation (create, update, delete) on the Spanner instance before timing out.
